title: Barbara Dissociation - Farmhouse Shock

selectedSequence.selectionReason: Barbara's farmhouse shock is the cleanest first proof node: close-up psychology, low movement, strong emotional reinterpretation potential, and minimal continuity risk.

emotionalObjective: Reframe Barbara's shock as dissociation: not louder terror, but the interior collapse after impossible violence. The original performance must remain visible underneath.
atmosphereObjective: Make the farmhouse feel airless and psychologically distant: thicker shadow density, colder grey highlights, subtle dust/haze, slight environmental pressure around Barbara, and the sense that exterior sound has fallen away.
continuityNotes: Preserve original shot pacing, blocking, camera geometry, and cut timing.
Do not change Barbara's facial identity, eye direction, mouth movement, or body position.
Do not add new characters, gore, modern objects, or stylized supernatural elements.
Maintain black-and-white 1968 independent film texture and practical-light realism.
Head and tail frames must match the original clip closely enough to splice back into the timeline without a visible continuity jump.
The original film must feel present underneath the reinterpretation.

audioContinuity.enhancementAllowed: very subtle room tone pressure only
